planning_request.user_request: Proceed from PRD/validation planning to the next phase: create a documented technical-options comparison and an implementation-ready MVP technical plan for the browser-based Training Sloop sailing simulator. Planning and documentation only; do not scaffold code, install dependencies, initialize Git, create a worktree, or deploy.
planning_request.desired_outcome: A grounded recommended stack and architecture with a staged, test-first implementation plan. The recommendation must support a deterministic, replayable 2D browser simulation, Sloop-only MVP, virtual water, keyboard controls, explanatory HUD/debrief, local-only replay, and PRD fail-closed validation/release gates.
known_context: MVP uses training-sloop-v1 only and required L01-L05 on synthetic virtual water. No code/repository exists yet.

Decision/replay contract requires versioned scenario/seed/input/model/profile/contract identity, deterministic state/event/score/debrief comparison, explicit coordinate convention, pause/reset semantics.
Current lesson registry dispositions are assumption. Implementation may use versioned simulation assumptions but product release remains blocked pending validation and P1 approvals.

Official docs observed in browser: Phaser is an HTML5 2D web game framework with WebGL/Canvas and JS/TS; Vite is a modern web build tool; Vitest is Vite-powered testing; Playwright is an E2E web framework across Chromium/WebKit/Firefox; PixiJS documents scene graph/render loop/ticker and scaffolding.
constraints: Documentation only. No code files, dependency install, Git operation, worktree, build, test, deployment, network service, or real marine-data connection.
Do not select or prescribe real sailing physics thresholds, polars, safety limits, or navigation behavior.
Keep simulation core independent of renderer/DOM; no backend/account/telemetry server in MVP.
Use TypeScript-oriented architecture and a test-first verification matrix, but pin package versions only at implementation bootstrap after a current compatibility check.
Separate permission to build a prototype from release eligibility; registry/P1 gates remain independent.
current_architecture: Documentation-only workspace; no source project, Git repository, package manager manifest, or test setup exists.
previous_review_findings: Renderer/engine must never own deterministic training state; state evolution, replay comparison, scoring causality and content versions need renderer-independent tests. Safety/domain registry gates block release but should not silently block a labeled prototype from being built.
